message:
fix: prevent castling while in check or through attacked squares (#4659)

The castling logic in KubeChess only checked whether castling rights
existed and whether squares between king and rook were empty. Per
standard chess rules, castling is also illegal when:
1. The king is currently in check
2. The king passes through an attacked square
3. The king lands on an attacked square
4. The rook is missing from its starting square

Added isSquareAttackedBy() helper with a skipCastling flag to avoid
infinite recursion, and updated the castling validation to enforce
all four conditions.

Fixes #4655

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>
